name: Y42 Analytics Engineering Platform API
what_it_does: Analytics engineering platform with REST API — enabling data teams to build ELT pipelines, SQL transformations, and data models with a Git-based version control workflow and collaborative IDE, functioning as an integrated alternative to Airbyte + dbt + BI tool, with built-in data source connectors, transformation layer, and data application publishing.
use_cases: Building ELT pipelines via Y42 API for source-to-warehouse data ingestion with 200+ connectors
Managing SQL transformation models via Y42 API for dbt-like analytics engineering workflows
Triggering pipeline runs via Y42 API for event-driven data model execution
Publishing data applications via Y42 API for self-serve analytics and dashboard delivery
Monitoring pipeline health via Y42 API for transformation job status and lineage tracking
not_for: Real-time streaming (Y42 is batch-oriented ELT and transformation)
Teams needing open-source self-hosted (Y42 is SaaS-only)
Simple source-to-destination ETL without transformation layer requirements
best_when: A data team wants an integrated analytics engineering platform combining data ingestion, SQL transformation (dbt-like), and data application publishing — avoiding separate Airbyte + dbt + BI tool stacks.
avoid_when: You need real-time CDC streaming, open-source self-hosted pipelines, or have a mature dbt workflow you don't want to migrate.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Y42 workspace settings. REST API at api.y42.com/v1/. Workspace ID required for workspace-scoped operations. Git-based version control: Y42 integrates with GitHub/GitLab for transformation model storage. Webhooks for pipeline events. Data source credentials stored in Y42 per-connector.

pricing.notes: German company (Berlin). Founded 2020 by Marius Moscovici and Cosmin Ochis. $20M+ funding (Sequoia Capital). Integrated platform: connectors + transformation + BI in one. 200+ connectors: similar coverage to Airbyte/Fivetran. Transformation: SQL-first with dbt-compatible model format. Git integration: GitHub/GitLab for version control of transformation models. Data applications: Y42 can publish interactive data apps beyond dashboards. Warehouses: BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift. GDPR compliant: EU company, German data residency option.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. Workspace ID scoping. Git-integrated transformation models. German company Berlin. GDPR/SOC2. 200+ connectors. Integrated ELT+transformation+BI. Annual contracts.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pipeline reads idempotent by pipeline ID. Transformation run triggers: check if already running before triggering. Model creation: verify by name before creating. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: Workspace ID required: all API operations are workspace-scoped — retrieve from Y42 dashboard settings
Git integration: transformation models stored in GitHub/GitLab — changes via git, not just API
Integrated platform: Y42 combines connectors + dbt-like transforms + BI — different mental model from standalone tools
dbt compatibility: Y42 transformation format is dbt-compatible but not identical — verify compatibility before migration
Data applications: Y42 publishes interactive data apps — distinct from traditional BI dashboards
German company: EU data residency available — specify region during account setup for GDPR

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token API key. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant (German company). EU data residency available. Source credentials stored in Y42 — warehouse and connector API keys. Git integration: ensure transformation model repos are private. Workspace isolation: separate workspaces reduce blast radius.
